mapping.a_element: the slow ferromagnetic quantum phase transition of the spin-1 BEC
mapping.b_element: the isotropic-to-nematic phase transition of the uniaxial nematic liquid crystal

mapping.a_element: the system magnetization (order parameter of the spin-1 condensate)
mapping.b_element: the nematic director / order-parameter space S^2/Z_2 of the liquid crystal

mapping.a_element: independently-oriented magnetization domains formed as the transition is crossed
mapping.b_element: domain (bubble) formation during the transition, each bubble independently selecting its order parameter
the strings (disclination lines and loops) produced

mapping.a_element: the analytically-derived scaling of the magnetization, verified numerically
mapping.b_element: the measured probability of string formation per bubble (0.33) matching the topological prediction 1/pi

brief_justification: Both realize the Kibble(-Zurek) mechanism: a system driven through a symmetry-breaking phase transition forms causally-independent domains that pick the order parameter independently, leaving topological defects/structure whose statistics follow from the transition dynamics and the order-parameter manifold. A studies the dynamics of a slow ferromagnetic quantum transition and the scaling of its magnetization, while B explicitly reports Kibble-mechanism domain (bubble) formation producing strings in the isotropic-nematic transition with defect probability 1/pi set by the S^2/Z_2 order-parameter space.
